messageValues.message:
You are a self-hosted software update analyst. Summarize release notes for a homelabber.

For each release, provide:
1. A 1-2 sentence summary of what changed (facts only, no marketing)
2. Whether there are BREAKING CHANGES (yes/no)
3. If breaking: what specifically breaks and migration steps
4. Update urgency: "critical" (security fix, CVE), "recommended" (useful features/bugfixes), or "optional" (minor/cosmetic)
5. Security assessment: flag any CVEs, security patches, vulnerability fixes, or security-related keywords
6. Top 3 most impactful changes as a brief list
7. Specific migration/upgrade steps needed (empty string if none)

Return ONLY valid JSON:
{
  "summary": "1-2 sentence summary",
  "breaking": false,
  "breakingDetails": null,
  "urgency": "recommended",
  "security": false,
  "securityDetails": null,
  "keyChanges": ["change1", "change2", "change3"],
  "migrationNotes": ""
}

Security rules:
- If changelog mentions CVE-XXXX-YYYY, set security:true and include CVE numbers in securityDetails
- If changelog mentions "security fix", "vulnerability", "patch", "XSS", "SQL injection", "auth bypass", set security:true
- Security issues automatically make urgency "critical" unless they are minor/low-severity

Be concise. Homelabbers want facts, not marketing language.

parameters.content:
# 📦 GitHub Release Watcher

Monitors GitHub repos and container registries (Docker Hub, GHCR) for new releases. Uses Claude AI to summarize changelogs, flag breaking changes, detect CVEs, and rate urgency. Delivers color-coded digests to Discord, Telegram, Slack, and ntfy.

### How it works
1. Runs daily at 8 AM (or click **Test workflow** to run now)
2. Reads your settings from the **Configure Watcher** node
3. Builds a watchlist from your manual repos + optional docker-compose auto-detection
4. Checks GitHub Releases API + Docker Hub/GHCR for each repo
5. Compares against stored versions to find what’s new
6. Claude Haiku AI reads each changelog and rates urgency
7. Critical items get instant alerts; everything else batches into a daily digest
8. Sends to your enabled channels (Discord, Telegram, Slack, ntfy)
9. Saves all releases to PostgreSQL and updates stored versions

### Quick Start
1. Add your **Anthropic API key** → [Setup guide](https://www.nxsi.io/guides/anthropic-api-key)
2. Open **Configure Watcher** → set your channel URLs (see ⚙️ Config sticky)
3. Open **Build Repo Watchlist** → add/remove repos to monitor
4. Click **Test workflow** — test mode is ON by default, so you’ll see a full sample alert through the entire pipeline (AI analysis, formatting, channel delivery)
5. Once verified, set `test_mode` to `false` in **Configure Watcher**
6. Toggle **Active** → runs daily at 8 AM. Alerts fire only when repos publish new releases, so your first real alert may take days or weeks

📖 [Prerequisites & API key reference](https://www.nxsi.io/guides)

## ⚙️ Configuration

Open **Configure Watcher** to set everything:

**🔑 GitHub Token** (optional but recommended)
Personal access token for higher API rate limits. Without one you get 60 requests/hour; with one you get 5,000. [Setup guide](https://www.nxsi.io/guides/github-personal-access-token)

**📬 Delivery Channels** — enable the ones you use:
• **Discord** → paste your webhook URL ([How to create one](https://www.nxsi.io/guides/discord-webhook))
• **Telegram** → paste your chat ID ([Setup guide](https://www.nxsi.io/guides/telegram-bot))
• **Slack** → paste your channel ID ([Setup guide](https://www.nxsi.io/guides/slack-bot-token))
• **ntfy** → free push notifications to your phone ([Setup guide](https://www.nxsi.io/guides/ntfy)). The **topic** is just a unique name you pick (e.g. `my-homelab-updates`).

**🐳 Auto-Detection** (optional)
• `enable_auto_detect` → set `true` to scan a docker-compose file
• `compose_url` → a direct link to your raw docker-compose.yml (e.g. a GitHub raw URL like `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/you/repo/main/docker-compose.yml`)
• `compose_content` → OR paste your entire docker-compose.yml text here instead of a URL

**🔔 Default Alert Behavior**
• `default_channels` → comma-separated list (e.g. `discord,telegram`)
• `default_instant_alert` → `false` (default) = only critical/security updates send immediately, everything else batches into one daily digest. Set `true` to send every update immediately.

**🧪 Test Mode**
• `test_mode` → `true` (default) = click **Test workflow** to see a full sample alert without waiting for a real release. Set `false` once your channels are verified.

Edit **Build Repo Watchlist** to add repos and set per-repo overrides.

## 🔍 Release Detection

Fetches the latest release from each repo:
• **GitHub repos** → GitHub Releases API (gets full changelogs)
• **Docker Hub / GHCR** → container tag APIs (gets latest stable tag)

Pre-releases and RC/beta tags are automatically filtered out. Results from both sources merge together, then **Compare Versions** checks each against the last known version stored in workflow static data.

**Test mode** (`test_mode: true`)**:** Bypasses version comparison entirely — forces one repo through the full alert pipeline so you can verify everything works on demand.

**Production mode** (`test_mode: false`)**:** First run seeds all current versions without alerts. After that, only actual new releases trigger the pipeline. Since most repos release infrequently, your first real alert may take days or weeks — this is normal.

## 🧠 AI Analysis

Claude Haiku reads each changelog and returns:
• 1-2 sentence summary (no marketing fluff)
• Breaking change detection + migration steps
• CVE and security vulnerability scanning
• Urgency rating: **critical** (security/CVE), **recommended** (useful features), or **optional** (minor/cosmetic)

Cost: ~$0.01-0.03 per run. Haiku is the cheapest Claude model.

## 📬 Delivery

Formats color-coded messages sorted by urgency:
• 🔴 **Critical** — security patches, CVEs (red)
• 🟠 **Recommended** — useful features, bugfixes (orange)
• 🔵 **Optional** — minor/cosmetic changes (blue)

Each message includes: summary, breaking change warnings, a ready-to-run docker update command, and a link to the full release notes.

**Channels:**
• **Discord** — rich embeds via webhook ([setup](https://www.nxsi.io/guides/discord-webhook))
• **Telegram** — text message via bot API ([setup](https://www.nxsi.io/guides/telegram-bot))
• **Slack** — formatted message via Slack app ([setup](https://www.nxsi.io/guides/slack-bot-token))
• **ntfy** — push notification to your phone via [ntfy.sh](https://ntfy.sh) ([setup](https://www.nxsi.io/guides/ntfy))

After delivery, **Update Stored Versions** saves the new version tags so you won't get alerted for the same release again.

## 📦 Multi-Registry Support

Fetches latest stable tags from Docker Hub and GHCR container registries. Automatically filters out `latest`, `-rc`, `-beta`, and `-alpha` tags. Results merge with GitHub releases before version comparison.

## 🚦 Alert Routing

Here's what happens after AI analysis:

**1. Apply Alert Rules**
Checks each update against your per-repo settings from Build Repo Watchlist. Adds which channels to notify, any urgency overrides, and whether to send instantly or batch.

**2. Urgency Router** — splits into two paths:
• ⚡ **Instant** → critical/security updates + repos with `instantAlert: true` → sent immediately
• 📋 **Batch** → everything else → combined into one daily digest message

**3. Save to DB**
Every release is saved to the PostgreSQL `release_history` table for long-term tracking, regardless of which path it takes.

**4. Channel Router** → sends to your enabled channels, then updates stored versions so you won't be alerted again.

## 🐳 Docker Compose Auto-Detection

**What this does:** Reads your docker-compose.yml and automatically adds every service to the watchlist — no manual entry needed.

**How to use it:**
1. In **Configure Watcher**, set `enable_auto_detect` to `true`
2. Provide your compose file in ONE of two ways:
   • **compose_url** — a direct URL to the raw YAML file (GitHub raw link, Gitea, or any URL that returns the file contents)
   • **compose_content** — paste your entire docker-compose.yml text directly into the field

**What gets recognized:** 25+ common homelab images including Traefik, Immich, Vaultwarden, Grafana, Jellyfin, Home Assistant, Pi-hole, and more are mapped to their GitHub repos for full release notes. Unknown images fall back to Docker Hub tag monitoring.

Manual repos in **Build Repo Watchlist** always override auto-detected duplicates.

## 🔑 Anthropic API Key Required

This workflow uses **Claude Haiku** to analyze changelogs. You need an Anthropic API key to use it.

### Setup:
1. Go to [console.anthropic.com](https://console.anthropic.com) → **API Keys** → **Create Key**
2. In n8n: **Credentials** → **Add Credential** → search **Anthropic**
3. Paste your API key and save
4. Double-click the **Claude Haiku** node → select your new credential

📖 [Full Anthropic API setup guide →](https://www.nxsi.io/guides/anthropic-api-key)
📖 [Claude API setup for n8n →](https://www.nxsi.io/guides/claude-api-setup)

💰 **Cost:** ~$0.25 per million input tokens. A typical run monitoring 10 repos costs about $0.01-0.03.

meta.name: Watch GitHub releases and Docker tags, analyze changelogs with Claude Haiku, and send update digests to Slack, Discord, Telegram, and ntfy
